subjects.name: Database Management System
prerequisites: Basic concepts of data structure and algorithms
subjects.objectives: To develop conceptual understanding of database management system for solving different industry level problems & to learn its applications.

outcomes.label: CO1
outcomes.description: Understand Database Management System, explain fundamental elements of a database management system.

outcomes.label: CO2
outcomes.description: Compare the basic concepts of relational data model, entity-relationship model, file organization and use appropriate index structure.

outcomes.label: CO3
outcomes.description: Apply efficient query optimization techniques, suitable transaction management, concurrency control mechanism and recovery management techniques.

outcomes.label: CO4
outcomes.description: Analyze the database design techniques and improve the design by normalization.

outcomes.label: CO5
outcomes.description: Design entity-relationship diagrams to represent simple database application scenarios, translate entity-relationship diagrams into relational tables, populate a relational database and formulate SQL queries on the data.

topics.title: Database Security Application Development using SQL
Different Anomalies in Designing a Database
Types of Single-Level Index: Primary, Secondary, Clustering

books: Henry F. Korth and Silberschatz Abraham, "Database System Concepts", Mc.Graw Hill.
Elmasri Ramez and Novathe Shamkant, "Fundamentals of Database Systems", Benjamin Cummings Publishing Company.
references: Fundamentals of Database Systems, Ramez Elmasri, Shamkant B.Navathe, Addison Wesley Publishing.
Ramakrishnan: Database Management System, McGraw-Hill.

subjects.name: Computer Networking
subjects.objectives: Understanding the basic concept of different network models, explaining the network architecture, analyzing and evaluating different network protocols.

outcomes.label: CO1
outcomes.description: Illustrate the network topologies, model and architecture.

outcomes.label: CO2
outcomes.description: Apply different networking device, protocol for problem solving.

outcomes.label: CO3
outcomes.description: Analyse different networking functions in different layer of OSI and TCP/IP Model.

outcomes.label: CO4
outcomes.description: Evaluate the optimal route for communication and idea about routing algorithms for data transmission.

outcomes.label: CO5
outcomes.description: Design network architecture and implement in practical field of work.

modules.title: Overview of Data Communication and Networking

topics.title: Introduction to Data Communication and Networking
Direction of Data Flow: Simplex, Half Duplex, Full Duplex
Physical Structure: Type of Connection, Topology
Categories of Network: LAN, MAN, WAN
Internet: Brief History, Protocols and Standards
Overview of Data, Signal, Transmission & Transmission Media
Circuit Switching: Time Division & Space Division Switch, TDM Bus
Multiple Access Protocols: Pure ALOHA, Slotted ALOHA
Internetworking & Devices: Repeaters, Hubs, Bridges, Switches, Router, Gateway
Congestion Control: Open Loop, Closed Loop Choke Packets
Quality of Service: Techniques to Improve QoS

books: B. A. Forouzan – "Data Communications and Networking (5th Ed.)" – TMH.
W. Stallings – "Data and Computer Communications (5th Ed.)" – PHI/Pearson Education.
references: S. Tanenbaum – "Computer Networks (4th Ed.)" – Pearson Education/PHI.
Black, Data & Computer Communication, PHI.
Zheng & Akhtar, Network for Computer Scientists & Engineers, OUP.

subjects.name: Design and Analysis of Algorithm
subjects.objectives: To study paradigms and approaches used to analyze and design algorithms and to appreciate the impact of algorithm design in practice, use different computational models, order notation and various complexity measures to analyze the complexity/performance of different algorithms.

outcomes.label: CO1
outcomes.description: Understanding the time complexity of the basic algorithms for the classic problems in various domains.

outcomes.label: CO2
outcomes.description: Apply the classic algorithms to solve different problems.

outcomes.label: CO3
outcomes.description: Evaluate existing algorithms by calculating the time complexity.

outcomes.label: CO4
outcomes.description: Design algorithm to solve various problems in different domains.

outcomes.label: CO5
outcomes.description: Design efficient algorithms for common engineering problems.

topics.title: Different Asymptotic Notations and Their Mathematical Significance
Minimum Cost Spanning Tree by Prim's Algorithm
Minimum Cost Spanning Tree by Kruskal's Algorithm
Branch and Bound Technique: Integer Programming
Disjoint Set Manipulation: Set Manipulation Algorithm UNION-FIND
Knuth, Morris, Pratt (KMP) Algorithm with Their Complexities
NP Complete Class and Their Interrelationship
Approximation Algorithms: Necessity of Approximation Scheme

books: A. Aho, J. Hopcroft and J. Ullman, "The Design and Analysis of Algorithms".
D. E. Knuth, "The Art of Computer Programming", Vol. 3.
E. Horowitz and Shani, "Fundamentals of Computer Algorithms".
T. H. Cormen, C. E. Leiserson, R. L. Rivest and C. Stein, "Introduction to Algorithms".
references: T. H. Cormen, C. E. Leiserson, R. L. Rivest and C. Stein, "Introduction to Algorithms".

subjects.name: Artificial Intelligence
subjects.objectives: Comprehend the fundamental concepts of Knowledge Representation and Inference in Artificial Intelligence and its utilitarian importance in current technological context.

outcomes.label: CO1
outcomes.description: Understand and explain the fundamental concepts of Knowledge Representation.

outcomes.label: CO2
outcomes.description: Apply difficult real life problems in a state space representation so as to solve them using AI techniques like searching and game playing.

outcomes.label: CO3
outcomes.description: Explore relevant literature and apply the concept of Heuristic Techniques of Artificial Intelligence to solve problems.

outcomes.label: CO4
outcomes.description: Develop Inference Models for proposing solutions to the problems of Artificial Intelligence.

outcomes.label: CO5
outcomes.description: Demonstrate and enrich knowledge to select and apply AI tools to synthesize information and develop models within constraints of application area.

topics.title: Architecture of an Artificial Intelligent Agent

modules.title: AI Problem Formulation as State-Space Exploration for Goal Searching

topics.title: State-Space Exploration Formulation for Water Jug Problem
Memory-Bounded Heuristic Search: Iterative-Deepening A* Search

modules.title: Knowledge Representation and Inference using Propositional Logic and Predicate Logic

topics.title: Propositional Logic: Knowledge Representation and Inference
Inference and Answer Extraction using First Order Predicate Logic
Strong Slot-and-Filler Structure for Knowledge Representation
Expert System: Introduction to Expert System
Characteristics of Expert System and a Case Study
Introduction of Executive Support System and Decision Support System
Bayesian Inferencing and Bayesian Belief Network
Overview of Fuzzy Logic and Inferencing
Introduction to Natural Language Processing: Basic Concepts
Basic Concepts of Machine Learning Model

books: Russell, S. and Norvig, P. 2015. Artificial Intelligence - A Modern Approach, 3rd edition, Prentice Hall.
Rich, E., Knight, K and Shankar, B. 2009. Artificial Intelligence, 3rd edition, Tata McGrawHill.
references: Padhy, N.P. 2009. Artificial Intelligence and Intelligent Systems, Oxford University Press.
Deepak Khemani, "A First Course in Artificial Intelligence", McGraw Hill.

subjects.name: e-Commerce and ERP
subjects.objectives: The objective of the course is to explain the characteristics and functions of electronic commerce including mobile commerce, fundamental characteristics of electronic markets, common business models used in B2C and B2B electronic commerce. To acquire an overview to ERP and the knowledge on related technologies. Skill to ERP Manufacturing Perspective and ERP modules and to examine ERP tools and understand the benefits of ERP.

outcomes.label: CO1
outcomes.description: Understand the policy issues related to privacy, intellectual property rights, and establishing identity those are germane to electronic commerce along with the Internet and related technologies.

outcomes.label: CO2
outcomes.description: Comprehend the underlying economic mechanisms and driving forces of E-Commerce.

outcomes.label: CO3
outcomes.description: Analyse the impact that electronic commerce is facing and outlines the different digital transaction process and basic concepts of e-commerce.

outcomes.label: CO4
outcomes.description: Identify different technologies and IT support used in ERP.

outcomes.label: CO5
outcomes.description: Apply different tools used in ERP.

topics.title: Risks: Paper Document vs. Electronic Document
Laws and Legal Issues for Internet Commerce
Search Engines and Intelligent Agents in E-Commerce

modules.title: Business to Consumer E-Commerce and E-Business

topics.title: Integrating Intranet and Internet Web Applications Across Multiple Networks
Internet Bookshops, Software Supplies and Support, Electronic Newspapers
Internet Banking, Virtual Auctions, Online Share Dealing

books: E-Commerce-Strategy, Technologies & Applications by David Whitley, TMH.
Handbook on Electronic Commerce, Shaw et al., Springer publication.
Enterprise Resource Planning – Alexis Leon, Tata McGraw Hill.
references: E-Commerce-Strategy, Technologies & Applications by David Whitley, TMH.
Applied E-Commerce, Langer, John Wiley Publication.
E-Commerce - The cutting edge of business by Kamlesh K. Bajaj, TMH.
Enterprise Resource Planning, 2nd Edition by Alexis Leon, Tata McGraw Hill Education, 2008.
Guide to Planning ERP Application, Annetta Clewwto and Dane Franklin, McGraw Hill, 1997.

subjects.name: Mobile Application Development
subjects.objectives: This course introduces students to programming technologies, design and development related to mobile applications. Topics include accessing device capabilities, industry standards, operating systems, and programming for mobile applications using an OS Software Development Kit (SDK). Upon completion, students should be able to create basic applications for mobile devices.

outcomes.label: CO1
outcomes.description: Identify various concepts of mobile programming that make it unique from programming for other platforms.

outcomes.label: CO2
outcomes.description: Critique mobile applications on their design pros and cons.

outcomes.label: CO3
outcomes.description: Utilize rapid prototyping techniques to design and develop sophisticated mobile interfaces.

outcomes.label: CO4
outcomes.description: Program mobile applications for the Android operating system that use basic and advanced phone features.

outcomes.label: CO5
outcomes.description: Deploy applications to the Android marketplace for distribution.

topics.title: Android Manifest File and Its Common Settings
Managing Application Resources in a Hierarchy
Working with Different Types of Resources
Using Android Data and Storage APIs
Sharing Data between Applications with Content Providers
Deploying Android Application to the World

books: T1. Lauren Darcey and Shane Conder, "Android Wireless Application Development", Pearson Education, 2nd ed.
references: R1. Reto Meier, "Professional Android 2 Application Development", Wiley India Pvt Ltd.
R2. Mark L Murphy, "Beginning Android", Wiley India Pvt Ltd.
R3. Android Application Development All in one for Dummies by Barry Burd, Edition: I.

subjects.name: Microprocessor and Microcontroller
subjects.objectives: To develop an in-depth understanding of the operation of microprocessors and microcontrollers, machine language programming & interfacing techniques.

outcomes.label: CO1
outcomes.description: Describe the general architecture of a microcomputer system and architecture & organization of 8085 & 8086 Microprocessor.

outcomes.label: CO2
outcomes.description: Understand the difference between 8085 and advanced microprocessor.

outcomes.label: CO3
outcomes.description: Understand and classify the instruction set of 8085 microprocessor and distinguish the use of different instructions and apply it in assembly language programming.

outcomes.label: CO4
outcomes.description: Recognize 8051 micro controller hardware, input/output pins, ports, external memory, counters and timers, instruction set, addressing modes, serial data i/o, interrupts.

outcomes.label: CO5
outcomes.description: Understand and realize the Interfacing of memory & various I/O devices with 8085 microprocessors.

topics.title: History of Evolution of Microprocessor and Microcontrollers
Advantages and Disadvantages of Microprocessors and Microcontrollers
Status Signals and the Control Signals
Timing Diagram of the Instructions (a few examples)
I/O Mapped I/O and Memory Mapped I/O
Serial Communication Using SID and SOD Pins and RIM, SIM Instructions
Interfacing of 8255 PPI with 8085 and Microcontroller 8051

books: Microprocessor Architecture, Programming and Application with 8085 – R. Gaonkar, Penram International.
Fundamentals of Microprocessor and Microcontroller - B.Ram.
An Introduction to Microprocessor and Applications – Krishna Kant, Macmillan.
references: Microprocessors and Microcontrollers - N. Senthil Kumar, M. Saravanan and Jeevananthan, Oxford University Press.
8086 Microprocessor – K Ayala, Cengage Learning.
Ray & Bhurchandi, Advanced Microprocessors & Peripherals, TMH.
The 8051 Microcontrollers – Uma Rao and Andhe Pallavi, Pearson.
The 8051 Microcontroller and Embedded System - Mazidi.
The 8051 Microcontroller - K. Ayala, Thomson.

subjects.name: Database Management System Lab
prerequisites: Basic knowledge about electronics and logic design
Basic knowledge of data structure and programming concept
subjects.objectives: To develop conceptual understanding of database management system for solving different industry level problems & to learn its applications.

outcomes.label: CO1
outcomes.description: Applying SQL and PL/SQL for processing database.

outcomes.label: CO2
outcomes.description: Analyze the database using queries to retrieve records.

outcomes.label: CO3
outcomes.description: Develop solutions using database concepts for real time requirements.

outcomes.label: CO4
outcomes.description: Students get practical knowledge on designing and creating relational database systems.

outcomes.label: CO5
outcomes.description: Understand various advanced queries execution such as relational constraints, joins, set operations, aggregate functions, trigger, views and embedded SQL.

topics.title: Data Definition Language (DDL) Commands in RDBMS
Data Manipulation Language (DML) and Data Control Language (DCL) Commands in RDBMS
High Level Language Extension with Triggers
Database Design using E-R Model and Normalization
Mini Project: Web Based User Identification System
Application Development using Oracle and Visual Basic

books: ORACLE PL/SQL by example. Benjamin Rosenzweig, Elena Silvestrova, Pearson Education 3rd Edition.
references: ORACLE DATA BASE LOG PL/SQL Programming SCOTT URMAN, Tata Mc-Graw Hill.
SQL & PL/SQL for Oracle 10g, Black Book, Dr.P.S. Deshpande.

subjects.name: Computer Networking Lab
subjects.objectives: Understanding the basic concept of different network models, explaining the network architecture, apply different computer routing algorithms in real life problems.

outcomes.label: CO1
outcomes.description: Analyse different network topologies and devices.

outcomes.label: CO2
outcomes.description: Apply the concept of different type of networking for implementation.

outcomes.label: CO3
outcomes.description: Implement and discuss the various services offered by transport layer such as TCP and UDP.

outcomes.label: CO4
outcomes.description: Apply channel allocation, framing, error and flow control techniques.

outcomes.label: CO5
outcomes.description: Apply the basics of networking protocols for solving real life networking problems.

topics.title: Familiarization with Different Networking Cables, Connectors, Hubs, Switches, Routers
Understanding IP Address, Subnet – Connect Computers in Local Area Network
Study of Basic Network Configuration Commands
Configure a Network Topology using Packet Tracer Software
Link Layer Error Detection (CRC) and Error Control Mechanism (Selective Repeat, Go Back N)
Implementation of Data Link Layer Flow Control Mechanism (Stop & Wait, Sliding Window)
Server Setup/Configuration: FTP, TELNET, NFS, DNS, Firewall
TCP/UDP Socket Programming: Simple, TCP Based, UDP Based Multicast & Broadcast Sockets

books: A. Forouzan – "Data Communications and Networking (5th Ed.)" – TMH.
W. Stallings – "Data and Computer Communications (5th Ed.)" – PHI/Pearson Education.
references: A.S. Tanenbaum – "Computer Networks (4th Ed.)" – Pearson Education/PHI.
Black, Data & Computer Communication, PHI.
Zheng & Akhtar, Network for Computer Scientists & Engineers, OUP.

subjects.name: Design Analysis of Algorithm Lab
subjects.objectives: To analyze and design algorithms, use different computational models, order notation and various complexity measures to analyze the performance of different algorithms.

outcomes.label: CO1
outcomes.description: Analyze algorithm to solve problems by iterative approach.

outcomes.label: CO2
outcomes.description: Analyze algorithm to solve problems by divide and conquer approach.

outcomes.label: CO3
outcomes.description: Implement algorithm to solve problems by Greedy algorithm approach.

outcomes.label: CO4
outcomes.description: Apply algorithm to solve problems by Dynamic programming, backtracking, branch, and bound approach.

outcomes.label: CO5
outcomes.description: Implement algorithm to solve problems by branch and bound approach.

topics.title: Implement Merge Sort using Divide and Conquer Approach
Implement Quick Sort using Divide and Conquer Approach
Find the Minimum Number of Scalar Multiplication for Chain of Matrix using Dynamic Programming
Implement All Pair Shortest Path for a Graph (Floyd-Warshall Algorithm) using Dynamic Programming
Implement Traveling Salesman Problem using Dynamic Programming
Implement Single Source Shortest Path for a Graph using Bellman Ford Algorithm
Implement 15 Puzzle Problem using Branch and Bound Technique
Implement any one using Backtracking: Graph Coloring Problem or Hamiltonian Problem
Implement any one using Greedy Method: Knapsack Problem or Job Sequencing with Deadlines
Implement KMP Algorithm for String Matching

books: Hopcroft and J. Ullman "The Design and Analysis of Algorithms".
D. E. Knuth "The Art of Computer Programming", Vol. 3.
E. Horowitz and Shani "Fundamentals of Computer Algorithms".

subjects.name: Artificial Intelligence Lab
subjects.objectives: Gain foundational knowledge of PROLOG to implementing Artificial Intelligent Agent as an executable computer program for Knowledge Representation and Inference.

outcomes.label: CO1
outcomes.description: Acquire foundational knowledge of PROLOG to implement an Artificial Intelligent Agent as an executable computer program for Knowledge Representation and Inferencing and understand the working principle of the agent and assess its utilitarian importance in current technological context leading towards lifelong learning.

outcomes.label: CO2
outcomes.description: Identify and formulate an engineering problem by analyzing its characteristics to fit a State-Space Exploration Framework or an Inferencing Agent Formulation Framework in Artificial Intelligence.

outcomes.label: CO3
outcomes.description: Explore relevant literature and apply the concepts of Artificial Intelligence to solve a problem by implementing well-known AI strategies using proper techniques and tools of PROLOG.

outcomes.label: CO4
outcomes.description: Develop ideas and propose expert systems offering solutions to the challenging problems of Artificial Intelligence.

topics.title: Week 1: Introduction to PROLOG Programming, IDE and Its Basic Components – Knowledge Representation and Inferencing
Week 2: Arithmetic, Boolean Expression, Decision Making Strategies
Week 3: Recursion and Looping through Recursion
Week 4: List of Data Items in PROLOG
Week 5: Blind Search Techniques – BFS, DFS (8-Puzzle, Water Jug Puzzle)
Week 6: Heuristic Search Techniques – A* Search (8-Puzzle, Route Finding Puzzle)
Week 7: Constraint Satisfaction Problem Solving (Graph Coloring Problem, 8-Queens Problem)
Week 8: Game Playing – Adversarial Search with Alpha-Beta Pruning (Tic-Tac-Toe)
Week 9: Discussion on Project Problems and Allocation (Problem Description Report Submission)
Week 10: Designing Solution Model and Proposal Report Submission
Week 11: Project Implementation, Verification and Documentation
Week 12: Project Demonstration and Project Report Review

books: Ivan Bratko, Prolog Programming for Artificial Intelligence, 4th Edition, Addison Wesley.
Russell, S. and Norvig, P. 2015. Artificial Intelligence - A Modern Approach, 3rd Edition, Prentice Hall.
Rich, E., Knight, K and Shankar, B. 2009. Artificial Intelligence, 3rd edition, Tata McGrawHill.
references: Padhy, N.P. 2009. Artificial Intelligence and Intelligent Systems, Oxford University Press.
Deepak Khemani, "A First Course in Artificial Intelligence", McGraw Hill.

subjects.name: E-Commerce and ERP Lab
subjects.objectives: Understanding basic concept of object-oriented programming and PHP framework, Explaining the client-side components, Applying the PHP web application development concept in web application development.

outcomes.label: CO1
outcomes.description: Understand the concept of PHP framework.

outcomes.label: CO2
outcomes.description: Analyzing different client and server-side components for developing application.

outcomes.label: CO3
outcomes.description: Apply different concept for developing MVC application.

outcomes.label: CO4
outcomes.description: Implement the solution to real life problem using PHP concepts.

outcomes.label: CO5
outcomes.description: Apply different tools used in ERP.

topics.title: Introduction to PHP: Evaluation, Basic Syntax, Variable Constant, Data Types, Control Structure, Function, Array, String
Web Designing: Introduction to HTML, HTML Tags, Creating Forms, Creating Tables, Managing Home Page, JavaScript, CSS
Database Connectivity with MySQL: Introduction to RDBMS, Connection with MySQL Database
Performing Basic Database Operations (DML): Insert, Delete, Update, Select
Setting Query Parameter, Executing Query Join: Cross Joins, Inner Joins, Outer Joins, Self Joins
Online Banking and Credit Card Transaction Processing
Database Connectivity with MySQL: Insert, Delete, Update, Select
Executing Query Join: Cross Joins, Inner Joins, Outer Joins, Self Joins

books: PhP Complete Reference Steven Holzner.
references: Programming PHP Kevin Tatroe.

subjects.name: Mobile Application Development Lab
prerequisites: Basic knowledge on programming and database concepts
subjects.objectives: This is a practical course on Mobile Application Development and student will learn how to program in Android Platform and develop applications using SQLite that run on Android Operating System.

outcomes.label: CO1
outcomes.description: Design and develop user interfaces for mobile apps using basic building blocks, UI components and application structure.

outcomes.label: CO2
outcomes.description: Write simple programs and develop small applications using the concepts of UI design, layouts and preferences.

outcomes.label: CO3
outcomes.description: Develop applications with multiple activities using intents, array adapter, exceptions and options menu.

outcomes.label: CO4
outcomes.description: Implement activities with dialogs, spinner, fragments and navigation drawer by applying themes.

outcomes.label: CO5
outcomes.description: Develop mobile applications using SQLite.

topics.title: Fundamentals: Activities, Services, Broadcast Receivers and Content Providers, UI Components – Views and Notifications, Intents and Intent Filters
Application Structure: AndroidManifest.xml, User-Permission, SDK, Resources and R.java, Assets, Layouts and Drawable Resources, Activities and Activity Lifecycle
Emulator-Android Virtual Device: Launching Emulator, Editing Emulator Settings, Emulator Shortcuts, Logcat Usage, Introduction to DDMS
Basic UI Design: Form Widgets, Text Fields, Validation of EditText, Layouts, [dip, dp, sip, sp] versus px
Preferences: Shared Preferences, Preferences from XML
Menu: Option Menu, Context Menu, Menu from XML, Menu via Code
UI Design: Time and Date, Images and Media, Android Adapter and ListView, Composite, Alert Dialogs and Toast, Popup, Fragments, Navigation Drawer
Tabs, Tab Activity Styles & Themes: styles.xml, Drawable Resources for Shapes, Gradients (Selectors), Style Attribute in Layout File, Applying Themes via Code and Manifest File
Content Providers: SQLite Programming, SQLite Open Helper, SQLite Database, Cursor, Reading and Updating Contacts, Reading Bookmarks

books: Joseph Annuzzi Jr, Lauren Darcey, Shane Condor, "Advanced Android Application Development, Developers Library", Pearson Education, 4th Edition (2015).
Lauren Darcey, Shane Condor, "Android, Wireless Application Development", Pearson Education, 3rd Edition.
Paul Deitel, Harvey Deitel, Alexander Wald, "Android 6 for Programmers, An AppDriven Approach", Pearson Education.

subjects.name: Microprocessor and Microcontroller Lab
subjects.objectives: To apply Assembly Level Programming for arithmetic-logical solutions and also to interpret the interfacing programming by conducting experiments.

outcomes.label: CO1
outcomes.description: Solve small assignments using the 8085 basic instruction sets and memory mapping through trainer kit and simulator.

outcomes.label: CO2
outcomes.description: Write 8085 assembly language programs like Addition, Subtraction, Multiplication, Square, Complement, look up table, Copying a block of memory, Shifting, Packing and unpacking of BCD numbers, Ascending order, Descending order etc. using trainer kit.

outcomes.label: CO3
outcomes.description: Validate the interfacing technique using 8255 trainer kit through subroutine calls.

outcomes.label: CO4
outcomes.description: IN/OUT instructions like glowing LEDs accordingly, stepper motor rotation etc.

outcomes.label: CO5
outcomes.description: Test fundamental of 8051 programs using the trainer kit.

topics.title: Familiarization with 8085 Register Level Architecture, Basic Instruction Sets (Data Transfer, Arithmetic, Logical, Branching) and Trainer Kit Components Including Memory Map
Familiarization with Storing, Executing and Viewing Contents of Memory and Registers in Trainer Kit 8085 and Simulator through Small Assignments

Programming using 8085 Kit and Simulator: Addition, Subtraction, Multiplication by Repeated Addition, Square, Complement, Look Up Table, Copying a Block of Memory, Shifting, Packing and Unpacking of BCD Numbers, BCD Number Addition, Binary to ASCII Conversion, Smallest and Largest Number from Array, Ascending Order, Descending Order, String Matching, Multiplication using Shift and Add Method
Program using Subroutine Calls and IN/OUT Instructions using 8255 PPI: Subroutine for Delay, Reading Switch State and Glowing LEDs, Glowing of Seven Segment Display
Program for Serial Communication between Two Trainer Kits
Interfacing of 8255: Keyboard, Stepper Motor Rotation
Study of 8051 Micro Controller Kit and Writing Programs

books: Microprocessor Architecture, Programming and Application with 8085 – R. Gaonkar, Penram International.
Fundamentals of Microprocessor and Microcontroller - B.Ram.
references: Microprocessors and Microcontrollers - N. Senthil Kumar, M. Saravanan and Jeevananthan, Oxford University Press.
8086 Microprocessor – K Ayala, Cengage Learning.
Ray & Bhurchandi, Advanced Microprocessors & Peripherals, TMH.
The 8051 Microcontroller and Embedded System - Mazidi.

subjects.name: Minor Project-I
subjects.objectives: Independent minor project to be completed by the student.
